CREATE TABLE Employees (EmployeeID INT PRIMARY KEY, Name VARCHAR(100), DepartmentID INT);
CREATE TABLE Departments (DepartmentID INT PRIMARY KEY, DepartmentName VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO Departments (DepartmentID, DepartmentName) VALUES (1, 'Human Resources');
INSERT INTO Employees (EmployeeID, Name, DepartmentID) VALUES (101, 'Alice', 1);
INSERT INTO Employees (EmployeeID, Name, DepartmentID) VALUES (102, 'Bob', 2);
INSERT INTO Employees (EmployeeID, Name, DepartmentID) VALUES (103, 'Charlie', 1);
SELECT EmployeeID, Name FROM Employees WHERE EmployeeID IN (SELECT EmployeeID FROM Employees WHERE DepartmentID = 2);
SELECT e.Name, d.DepartmentName FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DepartmentID = d.DepartmentID;
SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE e.EmployeeID = (SELECT MAX(EmployeeID) FROM Employees);
SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE e.DepartmentID = (SELECT DepartmentID FROM Departments WHERE DepartmentName LIKE 'Eng%');
CREATE TABLE Projects (ProjectID INT PRIMARY KEY, ProjectName VARCHAR(100), DepartmentID INT);
INSERT INTO Projects (ProjectID, ProjectName, DepartmentID) VALUES (1, 'Project A', 1);
INSERT INTO Projects (ProjectID, ProjectName, DepartmentID) VALUES (2, 'Project B', 2);
SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e INNER JOIN Projects p ON e.DepartmentID = p.DepartmentID WHERE p.ProjectName = 'Project A';
SELECT * FROM (SELECT EmployeeID, Name FROM Employees WHERE DepartmentID = 1) AS HR_Emp;
SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e ORDER BY e.EmployeeID DESC LIMIT 5;
